steps.title: Identify Moore's philosophical foundation
steps.content: The key signal is Moore's core claim that all people instinctively know certain things to be true—having a body, living in a world with objects—regardless of what they philosophically profess.

steps.title: Apply Moore's logic to skepticism
steps.content:
Choice D — By pointing out that Moore would assert that external world skepticism is at odds with other beliefs those proponents must unavoidably hold points out that skepticism contradicts unavoidable instinctive beliefs about bodies and the world that Moore identifies.

Choices A and B misrepresent the author's stance toward Moore (either as agreeing with critics or treating instinct as a barrier); C wrongly treats skeptics' instinctive proof-rejection as equally valid.

That matches Choice D — By pointing out that Moore would assert that external world skepticism is at odds with other beliefs those proponents must unavoidably hold.
